name: pr-prepare
description: Prepare pull request descriptions following project template. Activates on: prepare PR, create PR, pull request, ready for PR, draft PR, write PR
capabilities: Gathers git context including status, branch, commits, and file changes
Analyzes modified components and their purpose
Generates PR descriptions using standardized templates
Suggests PR titles following conventional commit formats
author: ByronWilliamsCPA
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation generator that provides workflow instructions for preparing PR descriptions. The static scanner flagged 56 issues, but ALL findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misinterpreted SHA256 cryptographic hashes as 'weak cryptographic algorithms,' standard git commands as 'shell execution threats,' and metadata URLs as 'network indicators.' The skill contains only documentation markdown with no actual code, no network calls, no data exfiltration, and no malicious functionality.
valueStatement: Writing clear PR descriptions takes time and consistency. This skill automatically gathers git context, analyzes changes, and generates standardized PR descriptions following conventional commit formats.
limitations: Does not push code or create PRs directly
Requires git repository with clean working state
Does not validate tests or linting automatically

useCases.title: Streamline PR workflow
useCases.description: Generate consistent, professional PR descriptions without memorizing templates or running git commands manually

useCases.title: Enforce documentation standards
useCases.description: Ensure all team PRs include standardized summaries, change lists, and impact assessments

useCases.title: Improve contribution quality
useCases.description: Help contributors write clear PR descriptions that make code reviews faster and more effective

promptTemplates.title: Basic PR request
promptTemplates.prompt: Prepare the PR for this branch. Include a summary, changes, impact, and testing notes.

promptTemplates.title: Feature PR
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PR description for this feature branch. What components changed and what benefits does this provide?

promptTemplates.title: Bug fix PR
promptTemplates.prompt: Prepare a PR describing this bug fix. Include what was broken, what changed, and how it was tested.

promptTemplates.title: Complex PR
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive PR description. Analyze all changes, identify breaking impacts, and suggest testing requirements.

bestPractices: Run the skill after completing changes but before pushing to ensure clean git status
Review the generated description and add any context-specific details
Use the conventional commit format for PR titles to maintain consistency
antiPatterns: Using the skill with uncommitted changes that are not part of the PR
Copying the output without reviewing for accuracy
Skipping the testing checklist items before creating the PR

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The skill works with Claude and Claude Code. It requires access to run git commands in your repository.

faq.question: What git commands are executed?
faq.answer: The skill runs git status, git log, and git diff to gather context about changes between your branch and the base branch.

faq.question: Can I customize the PR template?
faq.answer: The template in the skill is a starting point. You can modify it for your project or request changes to the skill template.

faq.question: Is my code data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No. The skill runs locally and only reads your git repository. No data is transmitted to external services.

faq.question: What if git commands fail?
faq.answer: Ensure you are in a git repository with changes to commit. The skill needs a clean working state or tracked changes.

faq.question: How is this different from GitHub PR templates?
faq.answer: GitHub PR templates are static files. This skill dynamically generates descriptions based on your actual code changes.
